revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 6 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月6日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 6일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 6 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月初六]][[zh:五月初六]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 6 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月6日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 6일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 6 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月初六]][[zh:五月初六]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 6 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月6日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 6일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 6 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月初六]][[zh:五月初六]]
{{Not to be confused with|May 6|qq=May 6}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月初六|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 6일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 6''' is the 6th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu|1=Butsumetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10886789}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1607|en|1607|WD=Q6756|lt=1607}} {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29}}) – A Korean envoy visited Edo for the first time.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://example.com|title=Reference needed|accessdate=2021-04|qq=}}</ref> * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 1st year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1865|en|1865|WD=Q7708|lt=1865}} {{ill|June 28|en|June 28|WD=Q2660|lt=June 28}}) – Under the mediation of Sakamoto Ryōma, negotiations with the Chōshū Domain led to the initial formation of the Satsuma–Chōshū Alliance.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://example.com|title=Reference needed|accessdate=2021-04|qq=}}</ref>
== Births == * [[承和 (日本)|Shōwa]] 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|835|en|835|WD=Q23894|lt=835}} {{ill|June 5|en|June 5|WD=Q2622|lt=June 5}}) – Heira Hiro, one of the four sons of Hira Takamochi of the Kantō Taira clan (died {{ill|928|en|928|WD=Q24297|lt=928}}). * [[:en:応保|応保]] 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1162|en|1162|WD=Q19667|lt=1162}} {{ill|June 20|en|June 20|WD=Q2666|lt=June 20}}) – Benchō, a monk of the Jōdo-shū (died {{ill|1238|en|1238|WD=Q5433|lt=1238}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|635|en|635|WD=Q31779|lt=635}} {{ill|May 27|en|May 27|WD=Q2587|lt=May 27}}) – Emperor Jomei, the first emperor of the Tang dynasty of Japan (also known as Li Yuan, the founding emperor of the Tang in China; born {{ill|566|en|566|WD=Q32609|lt=566}}). * {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|763|en|763|WD=Q30692|lt=763}} {{ill|June 21|en|June 21|WD=Q2671|lt=June 21}}) – the great monk {{ill|Jianzhen|en|Jianzhen|WD=Q714061|lt=Ganjin}}, a prominent Chinese Buddhist monk who founded the Japanese Ritsu school (born {{ill|688|en|688|WD=Q30443|lt=688}}). * {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1408|en|1408|WD=Q6138|lt=1408}} {{ill|May 31|en|May 31|WD=Q2591|lt=May 31}}) – Ashikaga Yoshimitsu, the 3rd shōgun of the Ashikaga Shogunate (born {{ill|1358|en|1358|WD=Q6140|lt=1358}}). * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587}} {{ill|June 11|en|June 11|WD=Q2617|lt=June 11}}) – Otomo Sōrin, a Sengoku daimyō of Bungo (born {{ill|1530|en|1530|WD=Q6429|lt=1530}}). * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 17th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1612|en|1612|WD=Q6771|lt=1612}} {{ill|June 5|en|June 5|WD=Q2622|lt=June 5}}) – Arima Harunobu, a Christian daimyō (born {{ill|1567|en|1567|WD=Q6642|lt=1567}}). * {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 7th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1694|en|1694|WD=Q7694|lt=1694}} {{ill|May 29|en|May 29|WD=Q2589|lt=May 29}}) – Mitsui Takatoshi, the progenitor of the Mitsui family, a prominent merchant (born {{ill|1622|en|1622|WD=Q6815|lt=1622}}).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|5|5|7|4|6|6|5|0506|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

{{Not to be confused with|May 6|qq=May 6}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月初六|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 6일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 6''' is the 6th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu|1=Butsumetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10886789}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1607|en|1607|WD=Q6756|lt=1607|1=1607}} {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29|1=June 29}}) – A Korean envoy visited Edo for the first time.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://example.com|title=Reference needed|accessdate=2021-04|qq=}}</ref> * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 1st year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1865|en|1865|WD=Q7708|lt=1865|1=1865}} {{ill|June 28|en|June 28|WD=Q2660|lt=June 28|1=June 28}}) – Under the mediation of Sakamoto Ryōma, negotiations with the Chōshū Domain led to the initial formation of the Satsuma–Chōshū Alliance.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://example.com|title=Reference needed|accessdate=2021-04|qq=}}</ref>
== Births == * [[承和 (日本)|Shōwa]] 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|835|en|835|WD=Q23894|lt=835|1=835}} {{ill|June 5|en|June 5|WD=Q2622|lt=June 5|1=June 5}}) – Heira Hiro, one of the four sons of Hira Takamochi of the Kantō Taira clan (died {{ill|928|en|928|WD=Q24297|lt=928|1=928}}). * [[:en:応保|応保]] 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1162|en|1162|WD=Q19667|lt=1162|1=1162}} {{ill|June 20|en|June 20|WD=Q2666|lt=June 20|1=June 20}}) – Benchō, a monk of the Jōdo-shū (died {{ill|1238|en|1238|WD=Q5433|lt=1238|1=1238}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|635|en|635|WD=Q31779|lt=635|1=635}} {{ill|May 27|en|May 27|WD=Q2587|lt=May 27|1=May 27}}) – Emperor Jomei, the first emperor of the Tang dynasty of Japan (also known as Li Yuan, the founding emperor of the Tang in China; born {{ill|566|en|566|WD=Q32609|lt=566|1=566}}). * {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|763|en|763|WD=Q30692|lt=763|1=763}} {{ill|June 21|en|June 21|WD=Q2671|lt=June 21|1=June 21}}) – the great monk {{ill|Jianzhen|en|Jianzhen|WD=Q714061|lt=Ganjin|1=Jianzhen}}, a prominent Chinese Buddhist monk who founded the Japanese Ritsu school (born {{ill|688|en|688|WD=Q30443|lt=688|1=688}}). * {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei|1=Ōei}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1408|en|1408|WD=Q6138|lt=1408|1=1408}} {{ill|May 31|en|May 31|WD=Q2591|lt=May 31|1=May 31}}) – Ashikaga Yoshimitsu, the 3rd shōgun of the Ashikaga Shogunate (born {{ill|1358|en|1358|WD=Q6140|lt=1358|1=1358}}). * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}} {{ill|June 11|en|June 11|WD=Q2617|lt=June 11|1=June 11}}) – Otomo Sōrin, a Sengoku daimyō of Bungo (born {{ill|1530|en|1530|WD=Q6429|lt=1530|1=1530}}). * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 17th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1612|en|1612|WD=Q6771|lt=1612|1=1612}} {{ill|June 5|en|June 5|WD=Q2622|lt=June 5|1=June 5}}) – Arima Harunobu, a Christian daimyō (born {{ill|1567|en|1567|WD=Q6642|lt=1567|1=1567}}). * {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 7th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1694|en|1694|WD=Q7694|lt=1694|1=1694}} {{ill|May 29|en|May 29|WD=Q2589|lt=May 29|1=May 29}}) – Mitsui Takatoshi, the progenitor of the Mitsui family, a prominent merchant (born {{ill|1622|en|1622|WD=Q6815|lt=1622|1=1622}}).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 6 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月6日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 6일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 6 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月初六]][[zh:五月初六]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 6 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月6日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 6일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 6 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月初六]][[zh:五月初六]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 6 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月6日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 6일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 6 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月初六]][[zh:五月初六]]
{{Not to be confused with|May 6|qq=May 6}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月初六|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 6일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 6''' is the 6th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu|1=Butsumetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10886789}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1607|en|1607|qid=Q6756|lt=1607|1=1607}} {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|qid=Q2659|lt=June 29|1=June 29}}) – A Korean envoy visited Edo for the first time.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://example.com|title=Reference needed|accessdate=2021-04|qq=}}</ref> * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|qid=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 1st year (leap May) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1865|en|1865|qid=Q7708|lt=1865|1=1865}} {{ill|June 28|en|June 28|qid=Q2660|lt=June 28|1=June 28}}) – Under the mediation of Sakamoto Ryōma, negotiations with the Chōshū Domain led to the initial formation of the Satsuma–Chōshū Alliance.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://example.com|title=Reference needed|accessdate=2021-04|qq=}}</ref>
== Births == * [[承和 (日本)|Shōwa]] 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|835|en|835|qid=Q23894|lt=835|1=835}} {{ill|June 5|en|June 5|qid=Q2622|lt=June 5|1=June 5}}) – Heira Hiro, one of the four sons of Hira Takamochi of the Kantō Taira clan (died {{ill|928|en|928|qid=Q24297|lt=928|1=928}}). * [[:en:応保|応保]] 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1162|en|1162|qid=Q19667|lt=1162|1=1162}} {{ill|June 20|en|June 20|qid=Q2666|lt=June 20|1=June 20}}) – Benchō, a monk of the Jōdo-shū (died {{ill|1238|en|1238|qid=Q5433|lt=1238|1=1238}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|qid=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|635|en|635|qid=Q31779|lt=635|1=635}} {{ill|May 27|en|May 27|qid=Q2587|lt=May 27|1=May 27}}) – Emperor Jomei, the first emperor of the Tang dynasty of Japan (also known as Li Yuan, the founding emperor of the Tang in China; born {{ill|566|en|566|qid=Q32609|lt=566|1=566}}). * {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|qid=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|763|en|763|qid=Q30692|lt=763|1=763}} {{ill|June 21|en|June 21|qid=Q2671|lt=June 21|1=June 21}}) – the great monk {{ill|Jianzhen|en|Jianzhen|qid=Q714061|lt=Ganjin|1=Jianzhen}}, a prominent Chinese Buddhist monk who founded the Japanese Ritsu school (born {{ill|688|en|688|qid=Q30443|lt=688|1=688}}). * {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|qid=Q1193078|lt=Ōei|1=Ōei}} 15th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1408|en|1408|qid=Q6138|lt=1408|1=1408}} {{ill|May 31|en|May 31|qid=Q2591|lt=May 31|1=May 31}}) – Ashikaga Yoshimitsu, the 3rd shōgun of the Ashikaga Shogunate (born {{ill|1358|en|1358|qid=Q6140|lt=1358|1=1358}}). * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1587|en|1587|qid=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}} {{ill|June 11|en|June 11|qid=Q2617|lt=June 11|1=June 11}}) – Otomo Sōrin, a Sengoku daimyō of Bungo (born {{ill|1530|en|1530|qid=Q6429|lt=1530|1=1530}}). * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 17th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1612|en|1612|qid=Q6771|lt=1612|1=1612}} {{ill|June 5|en|June 5|qid=Q2622|lt=June 5|1=June 5}}) – Arima Harunobu, a Christian daimyō (born {{ill|1567|en|1567|qid=Q6642|lt=1567|1=1567}}). * {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 7th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1694|en|1694|qid=Q7694|lt=1694|1=1694}} {{ill|May 29|en|May 29|qid=Q2589|lt=May 29|1=May 29}}) – Mitsui Takatoshi, the progenitor of the Mitsui family, a prominent merchant (born {{ill|1622|en|1622|qid=Q6815|lt=1622|1=1622}}).
